question: Which of the following is NOT a common email protocol?
explanation: FTP (File Transfer Protocol) is used for file transfers, not email. POP3, SMTP, and IMAP are all email protocols.

question: What are the fundamental building components of a computer network?
explanation: Nodes (devices) and Links (connections) are the fundamental building blocks of a computer network.

question: Which type of wired transmission medium has a core of refined glass?
explanation: Optic Fibre uses a core of refined glass to transmit data as light signals.

question: A ______ is a collection of computers and devices connected together to enable communication and data exchange.
explanation: A Network is a collection of computers and devices connected to enable communication and data exchange.

question: Which area network covers a small area such as an office or a home?
explanation: LAN (Local Area Network) covers small areas like offices or homes.

question: OSI stands for:
explanation: OSI stands for Open Systems Interconnection, a reference model for network communication.

question: Which of the following are examples of Network Topology?
explanation: Bus, Star, and Mesh are all examples of network topologies.

question: Which of these is NOT an advantage of the internet?
explanation: Bad impacts on health is a disadvantage, not an advantage of the internet.

question: Who invented the World Wide Web (WWW)?
explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989.

question: The identification given to a computer connection to a network is known as:
explanation: An IP Address uniquely identifies a computer's connection to a network.

question: The acronym FTP stands for:
explanation: FTP stands for File Transfer Protocol, used to transfer files over a network.

question: ______ represents raw facts, whereas ______ is processed meaningful data.
explanation: Data represents raw facts; Information is data that has been processed to be meaningful.

question: How many layers are in the OSI Reference Model?
explanation: The OSI model has 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, and Application.

question: _______ is a network security system used to protect computer networks from unauthorized access.
explanation: A Firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic.

question: What are connections between web pages called?
explanation: Hyperlinks are connections between web pages that allow navigation.

question: A _____ is a software program that allows users to access and view web pages on the internet.
explanation: A web browser is software used to access and display web pages (e.g., Chrome, Firefox).

question: In which year did Vint Cerf and Robert Kahn propose TCP/IP protocols to allow different networks to communicate?
explanation: Vint Cerf and Robert Kahn proposed TCP/IP in 1970 to allow different networks to communicate.

question: A _______ is a collection of symbols or codes used to format a document.
explanation: A Markup Language uses symbols/codes (tags) to format and structure documents.

question: What does HTML stand for?
explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language, the standard language for creating web pages.

question: What is the difference between XML and HTML regarding data exchange?
options: HTML is used for exchanging data, XML is not
XML is used for exchanging data, HTML is not
HTML can have user-defined tags, XML cannot
explanation: XML (eXtensible Markup Language) is designed for data exchange; HTML is for displaying content.

question: HTML programs are saved using which file extension?
explanation: HTML files are saved with the .html (or .htm) extension.

question: The href attribute in the link tag specifies the:
explanation: The href attribute specifies the destination URL/address of a hyperlink.

question: Which of the following is a valid CSS color code?
explanation: A valid hex color code has exactly 6 hex digits preceded by #, like #000000.

question:
What type of tag is
?
explanation: is a break tag (line break), a self-closing tag used to create a new line.

question: Which is the correct way to tag an image?
explanation: The correct syntax is with both src and alt attributes.

question: If you want to use a background color for your entire webpage, which CSS selector should you use?
explanation: The body selector in CSS targets the entire webpage body for background styling.

question: Which HTML element defines the title of a document (shown in the browser tab)?
explanation: The <title> element defines the document title shown in the browser tab.

question: Block elements are normally displayed without starting a new line.
explanation: This is FALSE. Block elements always start on a new line. Inline elements do not start new lines.

question: Which tag would you generally use to make an unordered (bullet) list?
explanation: <ul> creates an unordered (bulleted) list. Each item uses <li> tags.

question: Which tag is used when creating an ordered (numbered) list?
explanation: <ol> creates an ordered (numbered) list. Each item uses <li> tags.

question: Which character is used to indicate an end/closing tag in HTML?
explanation:
The forward slash (/) is used to indicate a closing tag, e.g.,

.

question: Which HTML element is used for the largest heading?
explanation: <h1> is the largest heading in HTML; <h6> is the smallest.

question: How can you create rounded corners using CSS3?
explanation: The CSS3 property border-radius creates rounded corners on elements.

question: What does CSS stand for?
explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets, used to style HTML documents.

question: What is the correct HTML for referring to an external style sheet?
explanation: The <link> tag with rel="stylesheet" and href attributes is used to link an external CSS file.

question: Where in an HTML document is the correct place to refer to an external style sheet?
options: At the end of the document
explanation: External stylesheets should be linked in the <head> section for proper rendering.

question: Which HTML attribute is used to define inline styles?
explanation: The style attribute is used to add inline CSS directly to an HTML element.

question: Which is the correct CSS syntax?
explanation: Correct CSS syntax is: selector { property: value; } — so body {color: black;}

question: Which CSS property is used to change the background color?
explanation: The background-color CSS property sets the background color of an element.

question: Which CSS property is used to change the text color of an element?
explanation: The color CSS property sets the text/foreground color of an element.

question: Which CSS property controls the text size?
explanation: The font-size CSS property controls the size of the text.

question: Which property is used to change the left margin of an element?
explanation: The margin-left property sets the left margin (outer spacing) of an element.

question: How do you select an element with id 'demo' in CSS?
explanation: In CSS, IDs are selected with a # prefix: #demo selects the element with id="demo".

question: What is the default value of the CSS position property?
explanation: The default value of the CSS position property is static.

question: Which HTML tag is used to create a multiple-line text input area for user input?
explanation: The <textarea> tag creates a multi-line text input field.

question: Which <form> attribute tells the browser HOW to submit the form data (GET or POST)?
explanation: The method attribute specifies how form data is sent: GET (appended to URL) or POST (in HTTP body).

question: What table element attribute adds a border to a table?
explanation: border="1" adds a visible border to a table. border="0" removes it.

question: Which of the following defines the content of standard table cells?
explanation: <td> (table data) defines standard content cells in a table.

question: The HTML _____ element is used to create an HTML form for user input.
explanation: The <form> element is the container for all HTML form controls.

question: Which input type allows for selecting zero or more of many choices?
explanation: Checkboxes allow users to select multiple options (zero or more) from a list.

question: Which input type lets a user select ONLY ONE of a limited number of choices?
explanation: Radio buttons are mutually exclusive — only one can be selected at a time.

question: The action attribute tells the form:
options: How to send the form's data
Where to send the form's data
When to send the form's data
explanation: The action attribute specifies the URL (where) to send the form data when submitted.

question: Which input type defines a field that should contain an e-mail address?
explanation: type="email" defines an email input field with built-in validation.

question: <input> is:
explanation: <input> is an empty (self-closing) tag — it does not have a closing tag.

question: In HTML5, which tag is used to initialize the document type?
explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> is the correct HTML5 document type declaration.

question: What is the decimal value of binary 11111110?
The Octal number system contains how many digits?
explanation: The octal system uses 8 digits: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.

question: What is the binary number for decimal number 9?
explanation: 9 in decimal = 8+1 = 1001 in binary.

question: People normally work in _____ and computers in _____.
explanation: Humans use the decimal (base-10) system; computers use binary (base-2).

question: Convert 123 (base 10) to binary.
Convert (1101) base 2 into a decimal number.
explanation: 1101 in binary = 8+4+0+1 = 13 in decimal.

question: Which numbering system uses both numbers and letters as symbols?
explanation: Hexadecimal (base-16) uses digits 0–9 and letters A–F as symbols.

question: Which of the following is NOT a binary number?
explanation: Binary numbers only contain 0s and 1s. '11E' contains the letter E, so it's not binary.

question: ___ is a base 8 number system.
explanation: Octal is the base-8 number system, using digits 0–7.

question: Convert (214) base 8 into a binary number.
_____ store and deliver requested data to clients.
explanation: Servers store data and respond to requests from clients.

question: Which attribute specifies the number of rows a cell should span in an HTML table?
explanation: The rowspan attribute makes a table cell span multiple rows vertically.

question: ALT attribute serves as a descriptive element for the image.
explanation: TRUE. The alt attribute provides alternative text description for images.

question: What is the practice of connecting computers together to enable communication and data exchange called?
explanation: Computer Networking is the practice of connecting computers to enable communication and data exchange.

question: A network node can be represented by which of the following?
options: Data Communication Equipment like a Router
Free space in a wireless network
explanation: A network node is represented by data communication equipment like routers, computers, or switches.

question: Which protocol translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses?
explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) translates domain names like google.com into IP addresses.

question: Which architecture features nodes that can be specifically designated as either Servers or Clients?
explanation: In Client-Server architecture, nodes are specifically designated as either servers or clients.

question: In which architecture is there no concept of a central server?
explanation: In Peer-to-Peer (P2P) architecture, all nodes are equal and there is no central server.

question: What is the primary difference between the Internet and the World Wide Web (WWW)?
options: The Internet is a service, while the WWW is a network.
The Internet is hardware-oriented; the WWW is software-oriented.
The WWW was invented before the Internet.
explanation: The Internet is the physical network infrastructure; the WWW is a service/software system that runs on top of the Internet.

question: Which phase of the web design process involves research and strategy?
explanation: The Discovery Phase involves research, strategy, stakeholder interviews, and user research.

question: What tool is used in the discovery phase to show the hierarchy and navigation of a website?
explanation: A Sitemap is a visual representation of a website's hierarchy and navigation structure.

question: What is a 'low-fidelity sketch' of a website that focuses on functionality and layout called?
explanation: A Wireframe is a low-fidelity sketch focusing on functionality and usability, not visual design.

question: Which phase of the web design process converts the final design into a working prototype using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript?
explanation: The Development Phase converts designs into working web pages using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

question: HTML elements are typically surrounded by which characters?
explanation:
HTML tags use angle brackets < > to wrap element names, e.g.,

,

.

question: Which part of an HTML element provides additional information to modify its behavior?
explanation: Attributes provide additional information about HTML elements, e.g., href, src, class.

question: In an HTML document, where is information like the title and metadata stored?
explanation: The <head> section contains metadata, title, links to stylesheets, and other non-visible information.

question: Which design principle involves arranging components to convey their importance?
explanation: Visual Hierarchy is the arrangement of elements to guide users' attention to the most important content first.

question: A website that adjusts to different screen sizes and devices is said to be:
explanation: A Responsive website adapts its layout to different screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile).

question: Which attribute is used with the tag to provide alternative text?
explanation: The alt attribute provides alternative text for images, shown when image fails to load or for screen readers.

question: What is the mandatory attribute for the <a> (hyperlink) element?
explanation: The href attribute is required for the anchor <a> element as it specifies the link destination.

question: Which protocol is the secure version of HTTP?
explanation: HTTPS (HyperText Transfer Protocol Secure) encrypts data transmission for secure communication.

question: Which organization developed the OSI 7-layer architecture?
explanation: The OSI model was developed by ISO (International Organization for Standardization).

question: Which topology refers to the physical layout arrangement of devices?
explanation: Network Topology refers to how devices are physically or logically arranged in a network.

question: Which of the following is NOT a common network topology?
explanation: Circle is not a standard network topology. Star, Ring, Bus, Mesh, and Tree are standard topologies.

question: Which OSI layer is responsible for the 'Presentation' of data?
explanation: Layer 6 is the Presentation Layer — responsible for data formatting, encryption, and compression.

question: Which protocol is used for sending and receiving emails?
explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) is used for sending emails. POP3/IMAP are used for receiving.

question: The origin of the internet is traced back to which 1960s project?
explanation: ARPANET (Advanced Research Projects Agency Network) was the first network, developed in the late 1960s.

question: Which technique breaks data into small units called 'packets'?
explanation: Packet-switching breaks data into small packets that travel independently across a network.

question: What is the 'root' element of an HTML document?
explanation: The <html> element is the root element that contains all other elements in an HTML document.

question: Which element is used to include a JavaScript file in HTML?
explanation: The <script> tag is used to embed or link JavaScript code in an HTML document.

question:
What does the

element define in HTML?
explanation:
The

element defines a paragraph of text in HTML.

question: Which tag is used to define a list item within a list?
explanation: <li> defines individual list items within both <ul> (unordered) and <ol> (ordered) lists.

question: Which type of network is hosted on public/private cloud providers and accessible on demand?
explanation: Cloud Networks are hosted on cloud infrastructure and accessible on-demand over the internet.

question: Which network device aids in communication between two separate network segments?
explanation: Hubs, Switches, and Bridges all aid in network communication, each at different levels.

question: Which attribute of the <link> tag defines the kind of relationship between the document and the linked resource?
explanation: The rel attribute defines the relationship, e.g., rel="stylesheet" indicates a CSS file.

question: Which layer of the OSI model follows the Physical Layer?
explanation: Layer 2 (Data Link Layer) follows Layer 1 (Physical Layer) in the OSI model.

question: The World Wide Web is a service that runs on top of the:
explanation: The WWW is a service/application that runs on top of the Internet infrastructure.

question: What is the latest version of HTML?
explanation: HTML5 is the current/latest version of HTML, introducing new semantic elements and APIs.

question: Which element specifies the connection to an external stylesheet in HTML?
explanation: The <link> element in the <head> section connects to external CSS stylesheets.

question: Which attribute of the <script> tag specifies the URL of an external JavaScript file?
explanation: The src attribute in <script src="file.js"> specifies the URL of an external JS file.

question: Which of these defines a data cell in an HTML table?
explanation: <td> (table data) defines standard data cells, while <th> defines header cells.

question: In the OSI model, which layer is responsible for routing data packets?
explanation: The Network Layer (Layer 3) handles routing, IP addressing, and packet forwarding.

question: What does the 'S' in HTTPS stand for?
explanation: The 'S' stands for Secure — HTTPS encrypts communication using SSL/TLS.

question: Which HTML tag is used to create a line break?
explanation: is the line break tag, a self-closing element that moves content to the next line.

question: Which tag is used to create a horizontal rule (line) in HTML?
explanation: <hr> creates a horizontal rule (thematic break/dividing line) in HTML.

question: The <title> tag must be placed inside which HTML element?
explanation: The <title> tag must be inside the <head> element to define the browser tab title.

question: Which type of network connects computers within a single building?
explanation: LAN (Local Area Network) typically covers a single building or small geographic area.

question: A 'Buyer Persona' is developed during which web design phase?
explanation: Buyer Personas are created during the Discovery Phase as part of user research.

question: What is 'Scope Creep' in web design?
options: The speed at which a website loads
Uncontrolled growth in a project's scope
explanation: Scope Creep is the uncontrolled expansion of a project beyond its original objectives.

question: Which tag is used to define a row in an HTML table?
explanation: <tr> (table row) defines a horizontal row within an HTML table.

question: What is the purpose of the action attribute in a <form> tag?
options: Specifies where to send the form data
explanation: The action attribute specifies the URL of the server-side script that processes the form data.

question: What does the method attribute in a form specify?
explanation: The method attribute specifies how data is sent: GET (appended to URL) or POST (in HTTP body).

question: Which tag is used to create a drop-down list in HTML?
explanation: The <select> element creates a drop-down selection menu in HTML forms.

question: Within a <select> element, which tag defines an individual option/item?
explanation: <option> tags define the individual items in a <select> drop-down list.

question: Which tag is used for multi-line text input in HTML forms?
explanation: <textarea> creates a resizable multi-line text input field in forms.

question: Which of these is a legitimate value for the target attribute in a link that opens a new tab?
explanation: target="_blank" opens the linked document in a new browser tab or window.

question: What is the character encoding standard typically used in HTML5?
explanation: UTF-8 is the standard character encoding for HTML5, supporting all Unicode characters.

question: Which metadata tag makes a website responsive on mobile devices?
explanation: <meta name="viewport"> controls the viewport settings for responsive design on mobile devices.

question: What does SGML stand for?
explanation: SGML stands for Standard Generalized Markup Language, the parent of HTML and XML.

question: Which markup language is known for being more 'strict' than HTML?
explanation: XHTML (eXtensible HyperText Markup Language) is stricter than HTML — all tags must be properly closed and lowercase.

question: An absolute hyperlink contains the:
explanation: An absolute hyperlink contains the complete URL including protocol, domain, and path.

question: Relative hyperlinks are useful for linking to:
options: Internal resources within the same domain
explanation: Relative hyperlinks reference files relative to the current page, ideal for internal site navigation.

question: Which HTML tag is used to create a line break?
explanation: creates a line break in HTML content.

question: Which OSI layer handles error detection and physical addressing (MAC addresses)?
explanation: The Data Link Layer (Layer 2) handles MAC addresses, error detection, and frame delivery.

question: Which device works at the Physical Layer (Layer 1) of the OSI model?
explanation: A Hub operates at Layer 1 (Physical Layer), broadcasting data to all connected devices.

question: What is the name of the first network that used packet switching?
explanation: ARPANET was the first packet-switching network, developed by DARPA in the late 1960s.

question: Who is considered one of the 'Fathers of the Internet'?
explanation: Vint Cerf, along with Robert Kahn, co-invented TCP/IP and is called a 'Father of the Internet'.

question: Which of these is NOT a valid HTML heading tag?
explanation: HTML only has heading tags <h1> through <h6>. <h7> does not exist.

question: Which tag provides a caption for an HTML table?
explanation: The <caption> element provides a title or brief description for a table, placed right after <table>.

question: Which element is used to group 'inline' elements for styling?
explanation: <span> is an inline container used to group inline elements for styling with CSS.

question: Which element is a 'block-level' container in HTML?
explanation: <div> is a block-level container used to group and style sections of content.

question: Which protocol is 'Connectionless' (does not establish a connection before sending)?
explanation: UDP (User Datagram Protocol) is connectionless — it sends data without establishing a connection first.

question: Which protocol is 'Connection-oriented' (establishes a connection before sending data)?
explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) is connection-oriented — it performs a handshake before data transfer.

question: In a 'Star Topology', all devices are connected to:
explanation: In Star Topology, all devices connect to a central hub or switch.

question: Which topology is the most expensive and complex to implement but highly redundant?
explanation: Mesh topology provides dedicated connections between every device, offering high redundancy but at high cost.

question: What is the main disadvantage of a 'Bus Topology'?
options: If the main cable breaks, the whole network goes down
explanation: In Bus Topology, all devices share one cable; if that cable fails, the entire network goes down.

question: Which input type is used for passwords to hide the characters?
explanation: type="password" masks the input characters for secure password entry.

question: What does the <label> tag do in HTML forms?
options: Defines a caption for an input element
explanation: The <label> tag defines a text label for a form control, improving accessibility.

question: Which tag is used to embed a video in HTML5?
explanation: The HTML5 <video> tag embeds video content directly in a web page.

question: Which tag is used to embed audio in HTML5?
explanation: The HTML5 <audio> tag embeds audio content (music, podcasts) in a web page.

question: What is the 'Application Layer' in the OSI model?
explanation: Layer 7 is the Application Layer — the topmost layer, closest to the end user (HTTP, FTP, DNS).

question: Which organization manages domain names and IP addresses globally?
explanation: ICANN (Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers) manages domain names and IP addresses.

question: What does 'UX' stand for in web design?
explanation: UX stands for User Experience — the overall experience a user has with a product or website.

question: What does 'UI' stand for in web design?
explanation: UI stands for User Interface — the visual elements users interact with on a screen.

question: Which tag is used to define the footer of a webpage?
explanation: The HTML5 <footer> semantic element defines the footer section of a page.

question: Which tag is used to define a navigation section in HTML5?
explanation: The <nav> semantic element defines a set of navigation links on a page.

question: Which tag defines an independent, self-contained piece of content?
explanation: <article> is a semantic HTML5 element for independent content like blog posts or news articles.

question: Which tag defines content aside from the main content (like a sidebar)?
explanation: <aside> is used for content tangentially related to main content, like sidebars.

question: What is the default port for HTTP?
explanation: Port 80 is the default port for HTTP connections.

question: What is the default port for HTTPS?
explanation: Port 443 is the default port for HTTPS (secure HTTP) connections.

question: Which HTML element is used to highlight text?
explanation: The <mark> element highlights text with a yellow background by default.

question: Which tag makes text bold in HTML?
explanation: <strong> makes text bold AND indicates importance. <b> makes text bold without semantic meaning.

question: Which tag makes text italic in HTML?
explanation: <em> makes text italic AND indicates emphasis. <i> makes text italic without semantic meaning.

question: What does the colspan attribute do in a table?
explanation: colspan stretches a cell horizontally to span multiple columns.

question: What does the rowspan attribute do in a table?
explanation: rowspan stretches a cell vertically to span multiple rows downward.

question: Which of the following is NOT a web browser?
explanation: Apache is a web SERVER software, not a browser. Safari, Edge, and Firefox are all browsers.

question: Which attribute is used to provide a unique identifier for an HTML element?
explanation: The id attribute gives an element a unique identifier used in CSS (#id) and JavaScript.

question: What is the file extension for an HTML document?
explanation: HTML documents use the .html or .htm file extension.

question: What does ARPANET stand for?
explanation: ARPANET stands for Advanced Research Projects Agency Network, the predecessor to the modern internet.

question: In what year was the first message sent over ARPANET?
explanation: The first message was sent over ARPANET in 1969, between UCLA and Stanford.

question: Which network topology connects all devices to a single central cable?
explanation: Bus topology connects all devices to a single shared cable (the 'bus').

question: What does PAN stand for?
explanation: PAN stands for Personal Area Network — typically covers a range of a few meters (e.g., Bluetooth).

question: What does GAN stand for?
explanation: GAN stands for Global Area Network — covers the entire world (like the internet).

question: In a Client-Server architecture, what is the role of the 'Client'?
options: To provide resources to other computers
To request services or resources from the server
To act as a central hub for all cables
To manage the network's security only
explanation: Clients request services/resources. Servers provide and respond to those requests.

question: Which architecture allows every computer to act as both a client and a server?
explanation: In Peer-to-Peer (P2P) architecture, every node can act as both a client and server.

question: Which layer is the 7th (top) layer of the OSI model?
explanation: Layer 7, the Application Layer, is the topmost layer, providing network services to applications.

question: Which layer is responsible for the physical transmission of data bits over a medium?
explanation: Layer 1 (Physical Layer) handles the raw transmission of bits over physical media (cables, radio waves).

question: Which layer handles routing and IP addressing in the OSI model?
explanation: The Network Layer (Layer 3) handles logical addressing (IP) and routing of packets.

question: What does ISP stand for?
explanation: ISP stands for Internet Service Provider — a company that provides internet access to customers.

question: What does DNS stand for?
explanation: DNS stands for Domain Name System — it translates domain names into IP addresses.

question: What does TCP/IP stand for?
explanation: TCP/IP stands for Transmission Control Protocol / Internet Protocol — the fundamental communication protocols of the internet.

question: Which tag is used to define the most important heading in HTML?
explanation: <h1> is the most important/largest heading; headings go from <h1> (largest) to <h6> (smallest).

question: How many levels of headings does HTML provide?
explanation: HTML provides 6 heading levels: <h1>, <h2>, <h3>, <h4>, <h5>, and <h6>.

question: What does WYSIWYG stand for?
options: What You See Is What You Get
Where You Sit Is Where You Go
Web Yields Standard In Web Graphics
Windows Yield System In Web Groups
explanation: WYSIWYG means 'What You See Is What You Get' — editing tools that display output as it will appear.

question: In a Description List, which tag is used for the term being described?
explanation: <dt> (description term) defines the term; <dd> (description detail) defines its description.

question: Which form method is more secure for sending sensitive data like passwords?
explanation: POST sends data in the HTTP body (not visible in URL), making it more secure than GET for sensitive data.

question: Which input type creates a button that clears all fields in a form?
explanation: type="reset" creates a button that resets all form controls to their initial values.

question: Which attribute makes an input field mandatory to fill out?
explanation: The required attribute prevents form submission unless the field is filled out.

question: Which type of CSS is written directly inside an HTML tag using the style attribute?
explanation:
Inline CSS is applied directly to an element:

Text

question: Which type of CSS is written inside the <style> tag in the <head> section?
explanation: Internal (Embedded) CSS is placed in <style> tags within the <head> section of an HTML document.

question: In CSS, how do you select elements with a specific Class?
explanation: Classes are selected with a period (.) prefix: .myClass { } selects all elements with class="myClass".

question: What does RGB stand for in CSS colors?
explanation: RGB stands for Red, Green, Blue — the three color channels used in digital displays.

question: Which CSS property allows you to choose the typeface (e.g., Arial, Times New Roman)?
explanation: font-family specifies the typeface (font) for text, e.g., font-family: Arial, sans-serif;

question: What are the four components of the CSS Box Model from innermost to outermost?
explanation: The CSS Box Model layers are: Content → Padding → Border → Margin (from inside out).

question: Which part of the CSS Box Model is the space between the content and the border?
explanation: Padding is the space inside the border, between the content and the border edge.

question: Which part of the CSS Box Model is the outermost space that separates one element from another?
explanation: Margin is the outer spacing outside the border, creating space between elements.

question: To use Flexbox in CSS, what value must the display property have?
explanation: Setting display: flex on a container activates Flexbox layout for its children.

question: Which Flexbox property aligns items horizontally (along the main axis)?
explanation: justify-content aligns flex items along the main axis (horizontal by default).

question: What does XHTML stand for?
explanation: XHTML stands for eXtensible HyperText Markup Language — a stricter, XML-based version of HTML.

question: What is the primary advantage of External CSS?
options: It makes the HTML file larger
It allows changing the look of an entire website by modifying just one file
It is faster to write than Inline CSS
It only works on modern browsers
explanation: External CSS separates style from content, allowing site-wide style changes from one .css file.

question: Which tag is used to group block-level elements for layout and styling?
explanation: <div> is a generic block-level container used to group and structure HTML content.

question: Which attribute in a <form> tag specifies where to send the form data?
explanation: The action attribute specifies the URL of the server script that processes the form submission.

question: The <fieldset> element is used to:
explanation: <fieldset> groups related form elements together, often used with <legend> as a caption.

question: What is the purpose of the GET method in HTML forms?
options: Sends data securely in the HTTP body
Appends form data to the URL
explanation: GET appends form data to the URL as query string parameters (e.g., ?name=value). Not for sensitive data.

question: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink (anchor) in HTML?
explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag creates hyperlinks in HTML. The href attribute specifies the destination.

question: Which of the following is used to define a hyperlink in HTML?
explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag is used to create hyperlinks in HTML.

question: Which of the following is NOT a type of CSS?
explanation: Remote CSS is not a valid type. The three types are Inline, Internal, and External CSS.

question: Which is the correct CSS syntax for setting a background color to blue?
explanation: background-color is the correct CSS property for setting background color.

question:
What is the default alignment of text in an HTML paragraph (

) element?
explanation:
By default, text in a

element is left-aligned.

question: Which tag is used to create a checkbox in an HTML form?
explanation: Checkboxes are created using <input type="checkbox"> in HTML forms.

question: Which of the following is a scripting language?
explanation: JavaScript is a scripting language. HTML and CSS are markup/style languages; XML is a data language.

question: What is the correct syntax to create an email link in HTML?
explanation: Email links use mailto: in the href attribute: <a href="mailto:someone@example.com">

question: Which protocol is used to browse the web?
explanation: HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol) is the protocol used for browsing web pages.

question: Which CSS unit is NOT a valid length unit?
explanation: gm is not a valid CSS unit. Valid units include px, em, rem, cm, pt, %, vh, vw etc.

question: What is the purpose of the <meta> tag in HTML?
options: To define metadata about an HTML document
explanation: The <meta> tag defines metadata such as charset, viewport, description, and keywords.

question: What does HTTP stand for?
explanation: HTTP stands for HyperText Transfer Protocol, the foundation of data communication on the web.

question: What is the default file name for a home page on most web servers?
explanation: index.html is the default home page file name recognized by most web servers.

question: Which of the following is NOT a valid HTML element?
explanation:
is not a valid HTML element. Use

, , or

for text content.

question: Which CSS property creates space INSIDE an element between content and border?
explanation: Padding is the inner spacing between the content and the border of an element.

question: What does VPN stand for?
explanation: VPN stands for Virtual Private Network — it creates a secure, encrypted connection over the internet.

question: Which HTML element is used to define important text (bold with semantic meaning)?
explanation: <strong> marks text as important (bold). <b> is just visually bold with no semantic meaning.

question: What is the correct syntax for creating a comment in HTML?
explanation: HTML comments use <!-- comment --> syntax and are not displayed in the browser.

question: Which of the following is NOT a network topology?
explanation: Grid is not a standard network topology. Valid ones include Bus, Star, Ring, Mesh, and Tree.

question: What is the purpose of the <canvas> element in HTML5?
explanation: The <canvas> element provides a drawable region that JavaScript uses to render 2D/3D graphics.

question: Which of the following is NOT a valid value for the target attribute in a hyperlink?
explanation: _middle is not a valid target value. Valid values are _blank, _self, _parent, and _top.

question: Which HTML element is used to display a progress bar?
explanation: The <progress> element displays a progress bar indicating task completion.

question:
How do you select all

elements with the class 'intro' in CSS?
explanation:
p.intro selects all

elements that have class="intro". Period (.) is the class selector.

question: What does the z-index property do in CSS?
options: Sets the transparency of an element
Specifies the stack order of elements
Defines the width of a box
explanation: z-index controls the stacking order of overlapping elements. Higher values appear on top.

question: Which CSS property is used to make text italic?
explanation: font-style: italic; makes text appear in italic. Values include normal, italic, and oblique.

question: What is the purpose of the opacity property in CSS?
options: To control the transparency of an element
explanation: opacity controls transparency: 0 is fully transparent, 1 is fully opaque.

question: What does the CSS property display: none; do?
options: Hides an element but still takes up space
Removes an element from the layout flow entirely
Disables all interactions with an element
explanation: display: none removes the element from the layout — it takes up no space and is invisible.

question: What is the correct way to apply a border to an element in CSS?
explanation: The shorthand border: 1px solid black; sets width, style, and color in one declaration.

question: What is the default value of the flex-direction property in CSS Flexbox?
explanation: flex-direction defaults to row — flex items are placed horizontally from left to right.

question: Which of the following is NOT a valid CSS color format?
explanation: CMYK is a print color model and is not supported in CSS. CSS uses HEX, RGB, RGBA, HSL, and HSLA.

question: What is the CSS float property used for?
options: To set the transparency of an element
To move an element to the back
explanation: float positions an element to the left or right, allowing text/content to wrap around it.

question: Which CSS property is used to control the space BETWEEN elements (outside the border)?
explanation: margin controls the outer spacing between elements, outside their borders.

question: Which value of the CSS position property places an element relative to its first non-static ancestor?
explanation: position: absolute removes the element from normal flow and positions it relative to its nearest positioned ancestor.

question: What does overflow: hidden; do in CSS?
options: Hides content that overflows the element
Expands the element to fit the content
Moves the element to the back
explanation: overflow: hidden clips any content that extends beyond the element's boundaries.

question: Which CSS property is used to add a shadow to text?
explanation: text-shadow adds a shadow effect to text. Syntax: text-shadow: h-offset v-offset blur color;

question: Which CSS property is used to change the bullet style of a list?
explanation: list-style-type sets the bullet/number style: disc, circle, square, decimal, none, etc.

question: What is the default behavior of position: static; in CSS?
options: The element follows the normal document flow
The element is fixed in place
The element is positioned relative to another element
explanation: static is the default position value — elements follow the normal top-to-bottom document flow.
